evaluations.feedback: This file provides an excellent introduction to Rust for JavaScript/TypeScript developers. It covers all fundamental concepts with clear explanations and practical examples. The ownership and borrowing sections are particularly well-explained. The tutorial could benefit from more advanced topics or real-world project examples to make it even more comprehensive.

This is a comprehensive and well-structured tutorial. It excels in covering a wide range of topics from basics to advanced concepts like async programming and concurrency. The examples are practical, and the explanations are clear and detailed. The inclusion of exercises with solutions is a great addition for learners. This is one of the best Rust tutorials for JS/TS developers.

This tutorial provides a solid introduction to Rust with a focus on practical projects. The explanations are clear, and the analogies to JavaScript/TypeScript are helpful. However, it could improve by covering more advanced topics like lifetimes and traits in greater depth. The project-based learning section is a strong point and offers valuable hands-on experience.

A well-organized and detailed tutorial that effectively bridges the gap between JavaScript/TypeScript and Rust. The explanations of ownership, borrowing, and lifetimes are particularly strong. The tutorial includes practical examples and exercises, which enhance understanding. It could be improved by adding more real-world project examples or covering async programming in more detail.

This tutorial offers a good introduction to Rust for JavaScript/TypeScript developers. It covers the fundamentals well and provides clear explanations. However, it lacks depth in advanced topics like lifetimes, traits, and async programming. The examples are basic and could be expanded to include more complex scenarios. The project example (TODO API) is a good addition but could be more detailed.
